Title: A DOF transcriptional repressor-gibberellin feedback loop plays a crucial role in modulating light-independent seed germination

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: gibberellins (bioactive GAs; GA1, GA4, GA5, GA6, GA7)
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DOF transcription factor; transcriptional repressor; DOF DNA-binding domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in dry seeds than in imbibed seeds; low in both light- and dark-imbibed seeds; GUS in vascular tissue of hypocotyl and cotyledons
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in dry seeds than in imbibed seeds; low in both light- and dark-imbibed seeds; induced by exogenous GA in dark and reduced by GA in light
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively fine-tunes GA levels during imbibition and restrains germination speed; loss of function increases GA/ABA ratio and accelerates germination in both light and dark
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 loss-of-function mutants (Chdag1-1, Chdag1-2); qPCR; pChDAG1::GUS reporter; GA/ABA quantification by UHPLC-MS/MS; germination assays under light/dark and PAC+GA treatments; heterologous complementation in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChDAG1 is a central repressor in Cardamine seed germination, acting independently of light to limit germination speed. 2. Loss of ChDAG1 strongly upregulates ChGA3OX1 and ChGA3OX2, increasing GA/ABA ratio in imbibed seeds. 3. ChDAG1 responds to GA in a condition-dependent manner, being induced by GA in darkness and repressed by GA in light, consistent with a GA–DAG1 feedback loop.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Not directly shown for ChDAG1; discussion notes possible differential epigenetic regulation compared with Arabidopsis DAG1, where PRC2-mediated control had been reported previously
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light versus total darkness during seed imbibition; exogenous GA4+7; paclobutrazol
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pChDAG1 (native promoter, endogenous/reporter); ChUBQ10::GAL4 with UAS system (constitutive transactivation context in related assay)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Chdag1 mutants show increased germination speed; under PAC plus exogenous GA, mutants displayed slightly reduced germination relative to wild type at 10 μM GA, indicating altered GA requirement/homeostasis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering improved germination under low-light or dark conditions by modulating GA homeostasis; requires caution because over-relaxing repression may alter hormone balance and germination behavior
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: When expressed in Arabidopsis dag1 mutant, ChDAG1 complements dark-germination phenotype but not light-germination or GA-response phenotypes, indicating partial functional divergence from AtDAG1

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low during imbibition in both light and dark compared with dry seeds
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low in imbibed seeds; constitutive overexpression via ChUBQ10>>GA2OX2 reduced dark germination
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased expression lowers endogenous GA levels and reduces germination in the dark
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transactivation overexpression (ChUBQ10>>GA2OX2) and dark germination assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChGA2OX2 acts as a negative component for dark germination by deactivating GA. 2. Its endogenous transcript level is low during imbibition, consistent with maintenance of GA required for dark germination. 3. Constitutive activation reduces dark germination, supporting the need to suppress GA catabolism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced germination under dark conditions upon overexpression
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid overactivation in breeding for robust emergence under low-light conditions; useful as a lever to reduce GA responses when suppressing precocious germination is desired
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic, this gene was functionally used here mainly as a manipulation tool to test the requirement for GA homeostasis during dark germination

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for germination-associated GA production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during imbibition, especially in darkness; increased only at 24 HAI in light
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in dark-imbibed than light-imbibed seeds; strongly derepressed in Chdag1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes maintenance of GA levels enabling light-independent germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in wild type and Chdag1 mutants; GA/ABA profiling; genetic inference from ChDAG1 loss-of-function
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChGA3OX1 is one of the major GA biosynthetic genes expressed in Cardamine seeds during imbibition. 2. Its expression remains high in darkness, unlike Arabidopsis, supporting dark germination. 3. ChDAG1 represses ChGA3OX1, and derepression in Chdag1 contributes to elevated GA/ABA ratio.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light versus total darkness during imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated expression may alter GA homeostasis beyond optimal range
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance germination under low-light conditions by sustaining GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding was not experimentally shown in this paper, but repression by ChDAG1 is strongly supported genetically and transcriptionally

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for germination-associated GA production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced during imbibition in darkness; increased at 24 HAI in light
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Markedly higher in dark-imbibed seeds and massively derepressed in Chdag1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports elevated GA biosynthesis required for light-independent germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in wild type and Chdag1 mutants; GA/ABA profiling; genetic inference from ChDAG1 loss-of-function
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChGA3OX2 is a major GA biosynthetic determinant in Cardamine imbibed seeds. 2. Its expression is high in darkness and is dramatically increased in Chdag1 mutants. 3. Repression of ChGA3OX2 by ChDAG1 is a key part of the GA feedback loop controlling dark germination.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light versus total darkness during imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression could disturb GA homeostasis and germination timing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for improving seed germination where light availability is limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The fold changes in Chdag1 mutants were especially large, indicating tight repression by ChDAG1

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: gibberellins (bioactive GAs; GA1, GA4, GA5, GA6, GA7)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: gibberellin biosynthesis and GA homeostasis during seed imbibition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-independent repressor that fine-tunes GA levels and is crucial for enabling proper light-independent germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restrains GA accumulation and germination speed in both light and dark; knockout accelerates germination and raises GA/ABA ratio
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; germination phenotyping; qPCR; pChDAG1::GUS; hormone feeding with GA4+7; GA and ABA quantification; Arabidopsis complementation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChDAG1 is the primary regulatory gene experimentally dissected in this study for controlling light-independent germination. 2. It represses the key GA biosynthetic genes ChGA3OX1 and ChGA3OX2, thereby mitigating GA accumulation. 3. GAs induce ChDAG1 in darkness, establishing a feedback loop that fine-tunes GA homeostasis during germination.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible cooperation with DELLA proteins is suggested by analogy and expression behavior, but no direct Cardamine protein-complex assay was shown
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in dry seeds; low after imbibition in both light and dark; induced by GA in darkness and repressed by GA in light
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in dry seeds; low in imbibed seeds regardless of light; vascular expression in hypocotyl/cotyledons; induced by exogenous GA in dark
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback loop: GAs induce ChDAG1 in darkness, and ChDAG1 represses GA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Not directly tested; discussion proposes possible epigenetic differences versus Arabidopsis DAG1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White light versus total darkness; exogenous GA4+7; PAC
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation changes germination kinetics and GA requirement; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis causes non-native regulatory outcomes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable regulator for tailoring germination under low-light or underground conditions, but dosage/context dependence and species-specific behavior should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike AtDAG1, ChDAG1 does not measurably regulate ABA metabolic genes in Cardamine and only partially overlaps functionally with Arabidopsis DAG1
